title: Bumble Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
text: Bumble Inc. announced its fourth quarter and full year 2024 results. Results were broadly consistent with guidance, with total revenue and adjusted EBITDA coming in at the high ends of their respective ranges, and Bumble App revenue outperforming guidance. Q4 2024 revenue was $262 million, representing a 4% year-over-year decline. For the full year 2024, the company expected total revenue in the range of $1,066 million to $1,072 million. The year 2024 represented significant challenges for Bumble. Bumble stock crashed nearly 40% after resetting its outlook for FY24, with the company now expecting only 1-2% year-over-year revenue growth for the year versus a prior expectation of 8-11% growth. Bumble shifted its strategy to require more robust profiles and pulled back on certain marketing investments, moves expected to impact near-term user growth and revenue but have a steadying long-term impact on the brand. The company moved to providing guidance on a quarterly basis, providing updates on product releases and marketing efforts throughout the year. For Q1 2025, Bumble expected total revenue between $242 million and $248 million (representing a 7-10% decrease), with Bumble App revenue between $198 million and $202 million (a 6-8% decrease).
